root_motivation: The laptop disk is 94% full with 205G of Rust build artifacts and no structured inventory — every reclamation decision today is a manual guess; ballast-survey makes the measurement first-class so subsequent deletion tools (ballast-reap, ballast-guard) can act on ground truth rather than ad-hoc du sweeps.
user_persona: Developer (Joe Yen) running automated build jobs on a single laptop that is frequently disk-pressure-bound; uses wintermute tooling daily and needs a machine-readable inventory to safely free space without guessing.

unfakeable_metric.name: acceptance_tests_passing
unfakeable_metric.harness_command: cargo test --workspace 2>&1 | grep -E 'test result' | grep -oP '[0-9]+ passed' | awk '{print $1}'

acceptance_criteria.description: `ballast-survey --json` against a fixture tree emits a JSON array of entries, each with `path`, `kind`, `bytes`, `entries`, `mtime`, `age_days`, `crate`.
Entries are sorted by `bytes` descending; the summary header reports `reclaimable_bytes` equal to the sum of entry `bytes`.
A nested reclaimable dir (e.g. a `node_modules` inside a `target`) is counted exactly once — the outer match wins, no double-counting.
`--min-size 100M` excludes every entry below the floor.
Age math is deterministic under `--now <rfc3339>` (no dependence on wall clock); a fixture with a known mtime yields a known `age_days`.
The tool never writes to or deletes any scanned path (verified: a read-only fixture dir survives a full run unchanged).
`--help` documents every flag; exit 0 on success, non-zero on an unreadable root with a clear stderr message.
`cargo test` green; `cargo clippy` clean on the crate's own code.

scope: Walk one or more root directories for reclaimable subtrees by rule (rust target/, node_modules/, .venv/, __pycache__, ~/.cache children, ~/.cargo/registry/cache, ~/.cargo/git/checkouts)
Size each matched subtree (apparent size), record entry count, mtime, age_days, owning crate/dir name
Emit structured JSON sorted by bytes descending with a summary header
Support --json flag for machine output, --root flags for multiple roots, --min-size floor filter, --now for deterministic age math
non_goals: Deleting or modifying any scanned file or directory
Integration with ballast-reap or ballast-guard (those are separate tools)
Supporting Windows or macOS platform-specific cache locations

additional.no_wall_clock_in_lib: All time-dependent logic must accept a caller-supplied DateTime; wall-clock read only at CLI boundary

five_whys_trace.q: Why do you want a disk inventory CLI?
five_whys_trace.a: The laptop is 94% full and du sweeps are ad-hoc — I need a structured view of what's safe to delete.

five_whys_trace.q: Why is a structured view needed instead of just running `du` again?
five_whys_trace.a: du output is per-human-run, unsorted by reclamability, and can't be piped to automated tools like ballast-reap.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does the automated tool need JSON rather than raw du output?
five_whys_trace.a: ballast-reap needs typed fields (kind, age_days, mtime) to make safe deletion decisions; raw bytes aren't enough.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should cargo target/ dirs be detected by name alone or require side-car files?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Detect by name `target/` AND (contains CARGO_OK or .rustc_info.json) OR (parent dir contains Cargo.toml) — reduces false positives on non-cargo target/ dirs.

ambiguities_resolved.question: What is the --min-size unit format?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Human-readable suffixes: 100M, 1G, etc. — parsed by humansize conventions.
